origin.name: Estonia
origin.key_document: Surmateatis (death certificate from Population Register, requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Estonia travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Tallinn guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Estonia is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is often simpler than full body repatriation. You will need the Surmateatis, cremation certificate, and urn transport documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Surmateatis (death certificate from Population Register, certified English translation required)
special_rules: Estonia is an EU member and Hague Apostille Convention member, which simplifies document legalisation.
Death registration is managed by the Population Register (Rahvastikuregister).
All Estonian-language documentation requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
The Prosecutor's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Estonian documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Tallinn can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Estonia to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Estonia. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Population Register, apostille, and documentation all handled. Contact us 24/7.
